skill.name: reducing-entropy
skill.description: Manual skill that guides AI agents to reduce total codebase size by loading a reference mindset, measuring before and after code volume, and favoring deletion over additions.
skill.summary: Guides deletion-first refactoring to reduce total code size.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline code formatting, ordinary prose, and reference links. No executable scripts, command invocation logic, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt-injection language was found in the reviewed files. The only residual risk is that the documentation includes external URLs and encourages aggressive deletion, so users should apply it with tests and review.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains External Reference Links
low_findings.description: The skill includes links to third-party articles, talks, PDFs, and books as source material. These are not contacted automatically by the skill, but users may leave the marketplace environment if they open them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown links under external reference sections. They are static documentation references, not network calls or exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: Aggressive Deletion Guidance Requires Human Review
low_findings.description: The skill strongly prioritizes reducing total code and rejecting changes that increase code size. This is useful for refactoring, but careless use could remove validation, logging, or compliance controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The deletion-first guidance is explicit, but the files also include limits for frameworks and compliance needs. This is an operational caution rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Command-Execution Matches Are Markdown Formatting
dangerous_patterns.description: The external command findings are false positives. The reported lines are Markdown code fences, inline filenames, or illustrative examples, and no executable Ruby, shell, or subprocess logic is present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual line review shows documentation syntax and examples only. No line contains an executable command runner or user-controlled shell invocation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Weak-Crypto Matches Are Prose False Positives
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by words such as simple, design, and description in prose and headings. No cryptographic algorithm selection, hashing API, password handling, or encryption logic is present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are natural-language guidance and frontmatter. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or related implementation code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Reconnaissance Matches Are Benign Directory Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The reconnaissance findings are false positives. The skill asks the agent to list and read files in its local references directory so it can select a mindset.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file-listing instruction is scoped to bundled reference documents. No host reconnaissance, network probing, or environment enumeration instruction was found.

content.user_title: Reduce Codebase Entropy With Deletion-First Reviews
content.value_statement: Codebases grow when every change adds files, helpers, and abstractions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to measure the final codebase and find safe deletions first.
actual_capabilities: Loads a relevant reference mindset before starting a simplification review.
Frames code changes around the smallest final codebase that solves the problem.
Compares before and after code size, file count, and function count.
Identifies obsolete functions, files, abstractions, and duplicated workflows for removal.
Flags common arguments that increase code without clear value.
Documents when deletion-first reasoning should not override framework or compliance needs.
limitations: It is manual-only and should be activated only when the user asks for it.
It does not execute tests or measure code size by itself without tool support.
It can over-prioritize fewer lines if the reviewer ignores safety, clarity, or compliance needs.
It is best for existing codebases and is less useful for net-new product development.

use_cases.title: Refactor an Overgrown Module
use_cases.description: Find duplicated helpers, shallow abstractions, and obsolete paths before rewriting a module.

use_cases.title: Review a Feature Request
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether a requested feature can be solved by deleting, merging, or reusing existing behavior.

use_cases.title: Plan Technical Debt Cleanup
use_cases.description: Rank cleanup work by the amount of code, files, and concepts that can be safely removed.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Entropy Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you want a quick simplification pass on one file or module.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the reducing-entropy skill on this file. Load one relevant reference mindset, explain its core principle, and list what can be deleted before adding code.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Implementations
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when choosing between a small patch and a deeper simplification.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply reducing-entropy reasoning to these two approaches. Compare final file count, line count, function count, and obsolete code removed by each option.

prompt_templates.title: Feature Request Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before accepting work that may add new abstractions or workflows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature request with the reducing-entropy skill. Identify whether existing behavior can be merged, removed, or simplified instead of adding new code.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Architecture Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for a multi-module cleanup plan that needs tradeoff analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a deletion-first architecture review. Load the most relevant mindset, map current abstractions, propose a smaller final design, and note safety checks required before removal.

output_examples.input: Reduce entropy in five form validation flows.
output_examples.output: The review recommends merging shared validation into one path, deleting three duplicated helpers, and keeping only checks used by active forms.

output_examples.input: Evaluate whether to add a new settings abstraction.
output_examples.output: The skill favors plain data and existing access functions because the new abstraction adds a concept without removing code elsewhere.

output_examples.input: Clean up a legacy module with many unused branches.
output_examples.output: The result is a deletion plan that removes dead branches first, then measures whether any replacement code is still needed.

best_practices: Load one reference mindset before reviewing code, then state the principle guiding the review.
Measure the final codebase, including files, functions, and concepts removed.
Run tests and review security, compliance, and data retention requirements before deleting safeguards.
anti_patterns: Using the skill automatically when the user did not request deletion-first review.
Treating fewer lines as better when it hides behavior or removes required controls.
Adding new abstractions because they feel organized without deleting more code than they add.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when you want to reduce code size, simplify architecture, or challenge a change that adds new files or abstractions.

faq.question: Does this skill write code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a review framework. The agent still needs normal editing tools and user approval for changes.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Will it always choose the shortest solution?
faq.answer: No. It favors less code, but it warns against ignoring framework conventions, compliance requirements, and necessary safeguards.

faq.question: What makes it different from a normal refactor?
faq.answer: It measures success by the final codebase size, not by how small the immediate patch is.

faq.question: Does it depend on external services?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only. It includes external reference links, but it does not call network services.
